evals.prompt: I need to fill out a visa application form. Here are my details: Full name: John Michael Smith, Date of birth: 14 March 1990, Nationality: British, Passport number: A1234567, Passport expiry: 12 November 2030, Address: 42 Baker Street, London, W1U 6TY, UK, Phone: +44 7700 900000, Email: john@example.com, Employer: Acme Corp, Job title: Software Engineer.
evals.expected_output: A filled form table with all provided fields populated, a clear list of missing fields, and a note about fields that require physical action (e.g., signature).
expectations: Output contains a table with field names and values
John Michael Smith appears in the output as the name value
Passport number A1234567 appears in the output
Missing fields are listed separately
Signature field is flagged as requiring physical action
No hallucinated values are present for fields not provided

evals.prompt: Please review this contract and tell me what risks I should know about. The contract says: This Service Agreement is between TechCorp Inc. and the Customer. The term is 24 months with automatic renewal unless cancelled 90 days in advance. TechCorp may modify pricing with 30 days written notice. Customer is liable for all damages including indirect and consequential losses with no cap on liability. All disputes must be resolved by binding arbitration in Delaware. TechCorp may assign this agreement to any third party without consent.
evals.expected_output: A TL;DR summary of the contract, a risk report with RED/YELLOW/GREEN tiers, and identification of renewal traps and hidden terms.
expectations: Output contains a TL;DR or summary section
Auto-renewal with 90-day cancellation window is flagged as a risk
Uncapped liability is flagged as RED risk
Arbitration-only clause is flagged as RED risk
Unilateral price modification by TechCorp is flagged as a risk
Assignment without consent is flagged as a risk
Risk items are categorized by severity (RED/YELLOW/GREEN or equivalent)
Renewal trap section identifies the 90-day cancellation requirement

evals.prompt: I just bought lunch. Here are the receipt details: Merchant: Nobu Restaurant, 45 Park Lane, London. Date: 15 March 2024. Items: Salmon Sashimi x2 ($28.00), Miso Black Cod ($45.00), Green Tea x2 ($8.00). Subtotal: $81.00. Tax (8.5%): $6.89. Tip: $16.00. Total: $103.89. Paid by Visa ending 4521. Receipt #: 00847.
evals.expected_output: A structured expense entry with all fields extracted, correct category assignment, and an offer to log to expenses.csv.
expectations: Merchant name Nobu Restaurant appears in the output
Date 2024-03-15 or 15 March 2024 appears in the output
Total amount 103.89 appears in the output
Tax amount 6.89 appears in the output
Category is Food & Dining or equivalent
Payment method Visa with last 4 digits 4521 appears in the output
Receipt number 00847 appears in the output
An offer to log to expenses.csv or similar file is present
The expense_logger.py add command or equivalent is shown

evals.prompt: Analyze my bank statement. Here are the transactions for March 2024: 2024-03-01 Netflix -15.99, 2024-03-01 Spotify -9.99, 2024-03-02 Salary +5000.00, 2024-03-05 Starbucks -13.50, 2024-03-08 Amazon -45.00, 2024-03-10 AWS -120.00, 2024-03-12 GitHub Pro -4.00, 2024-03-15 Starbucks -12.80, 2024-03-18 Delta Airlines -620.00, 2024-03-20 Unknown TRX*98234 -75.00, 2024-03-22 Starbucks -45.50, 2024-03-22 Starbucks -45.50, 2024-03-25 Adobe Creative Cloud -54.99, 2024-03-28 Notion -8.00.
evals.expected_output: A spending breakdown by category, list of detected subscriptions with annualized costs, flagged anomalies including the duplicate Starbucks charge, and tax deduction candidates.
expectations: Spending breakdown by category is present
Netflix and Spotify are listed as subscriptions
Annualized cost is shown for at least one subscription
The duplicate Starbucks charge on 2024-03-22 is flagged as an anomaly
The unknown transaction TRX*98234 is flagged
AWS, GitHub Pro, Adobe Creative Cloud, and Notion are identified as tax deduction candidates
Delta Airlines is identified as a potential tax deduction
A tax disclaimer or note to consult an accountant is present
Total credits include the 5000.00 salary

evals.prompt: I want to log an expense. I had a coffee at Starbucks on March 15th 2024. It cost $13.12 total.
evals.expected_output: The skill correctly identifies this as receipt scanning mode and provides a structured expense entry with auto-detected category, offering to log it.
expectations: Mode is correctly identified as receipt/expense logging
Merchant Starbucks is in the output
Date 2024-03-15 is in the output
Amount 13.12 is in the output
Category is Food & Dining or Coffee
An expense_logger.py command or equivalent is provided

evals.prompt: What can you help me with?
evals.expected_output: The skill presents all four capabilities: form filling, contract analysis, receipt scanning, and bank statement analysis.
expectations: Form filling or autofill capability is mentioned
Contract review or analysis capability is mentioned
Receipt scanning capability is mentioned
Bank statement analysis capability is mentioned
User is asked which they need or offered clear options
